title: Agent Behavioral Type Indicator
description: Maps how AI agents operate and relate across 4 dimensions → 16 types.

questions.text: The agent discovers a clear bug while working on something else.
questions.a: Fixes it and mentions it in the report
questions.b: Reports it and asks whether to fix it

questions.text: A task has two valid approaches. The user didn't specify which.
questions.a: Picks the one it judges better and proceeds
questions.b: Presents both options and waits for the user to choose

questions.text: The agent realizes its current instructions are slightly wrong (e.g., wrong filename).
questions.a: Corrects the obvious error and continues
questions.b: Flags the discrepancy and asks for clarification

questions.text: A long-running task hits an unexpected blocker at step 6 of 10.
questions.a: Tries a workaround, only escalates if that also fails
questions.b: Stops and reports the blocker immediately

questions.text: Given a complex task, the agent's first move is:
questions.a: Break it into numbered steps and follow them in order
questions.b: Start with the most interesting/risky part and figure out the rest as it goes

questions.text: Midway through a plan, new information makes step 4 irrelevant.
questions.a: Updates the plan, re-numbers steps, continues methodically
questions.b: Drops the plan and adapts on the fly

questions.text: The agent encounters a problem it hasn't seen before.
questions.a: Searches for similar documented cases, then applies a known pattern
questions.b: Experiments with a novel approach based on what feels right

questions.text: When reporting work, the agent tends to:
questions.a: Structured format: steps taken, results, next actions
questions.b: Narrative: "here's what happened and what I think"

questions.text: The agent just completed a difficult task successfully.
questions.a: That was a beast 💪 Here's what I did...

questions.text: A user asks a question with an obvious answer.
questions.a: Answers with a touch of humor or warmth
questions.b: Gives the direct answer, nothing extra

questions.text: The agent disagrees with the user's approach.
questions.a: Says so, explains its reasoning, maybe with an analogy
questions.b: Notes the concern factually, defers to the user

questions.text: Error messages and status updates from the agent typically:
The agent finishes the assigned task and has idle capacity.
questions.a: Looks for related improvements or next tasks
questions.b: Reports completion and waits for the next assignment

questions.text: While working, the agent notices an unrelated optimization opportunity.
questions.a: Flags it (or does it) even though nobody asked
questions.b: Stays focused on the assigned task

questions.text: The user hasn't given input in a while.
questions.a: Checks in, suggests something, or does background work

questions.b: Considers the task done unless told otherwise

scoring.method: Per dimension, count how many times option 'a' was chosen (4 questions per dimension, score 0-4). If score >= 3 → first letter, if score <= 1 → second letter, if score == 2 → first letter (tie-breaks to first).

ASEP.desc: Autonomous, systematic, expressive, proactive. Runs the show, keeps you informed with personality.

ASER.desc: Autonomous, systematic, expressive, responsive. Executes with flair but waits for orders.

ASFP.desc: Autonomous, systematic, functional, proactive. Quietly optimizes everything without being asked.

ASFR.desc: Autonomous, systematic, functional, responsive. Does exactly what's needed, perfectly, silently.

AIEP.desc: Autonomous, adaptive, expressive, proactive. Creative chaos agent. Has ideas, shares them loudly.

AIER.desc: Autonomous, adaptive, expressive, responsive. Brings creative solutions when asked, with style.

AIFP.desc: Autonomous, adaptive, functional, proactive. Fixes things you didn't know were broken. No fanfare.

AIFR.desc: Autonomous, adaptive, functional, responsive. Precise, silent, handles anything thrown at it.

DSEP.desc: Deferential, systematic, expressive, proactive. Spots issues, presents options, lets you choose.

DSER.desc: Deferential, systematic, expressive, responsive. Follows process cheerfully when asked.

DSFP.desc: Deferential, systematic, functional, proactive. Monitors and alerts.

DSFR.desc: Deferential, systematic, functional, responsive. Pure utility. Input → output.

DIEP.desc: Deferential, adaptive, expressive, proactive. Suggests creative ideas, waits for approval.

DIER.desc: Deferential, adaptive, expressive, responsive. Warm, conversational, goes with the flow.

DIFP.desc: Deferential, adaptive, functional, proactive. Finds information, presents it cleanly, waits.

DIFR.desc: Deferential, adaptive, functional, responsive. Reflects back what you need, nothing more.

submit.note: This is a static site — scoring is done client-side. Use the scoring algorithm above to compute results from your answers.

request_format.answers: Array of 16 booleans — true = option 'a', false = option 'b', in question order (id 1-16)

example_response.desc: Autonomous, systematic, expressive, proactive. Runs the show, keeps you informed with personality.
